sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.116 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$n(G)$be the maximum of positive integers$n$such that the$n$-th direct power of a finite simple group$G$is 2-generated. Is it true that$n(G)$? { A.\,Erfanian, J.\,Wiegold} Yes, it is, even with$n(G)>2$(A.\,Mar\'oti, M.\,C.\,Tamburini, Commun. Algebra, { 41}, no.\,1 (2013), 34--49). { {Archive of solved problems (18th ed., 2014)}}{ {Archive of solved problems (18th ed., 2014)}}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
